changelog: first_boot.sh: fixed apply_usb_host_mode_fix() raw-mounting /dev/mmcblk0p1 on every boot to run its idempotency check, which raced the host's own already-active mount of that device and produced a harmless but alarming 'Can't open blockdev' kernel message on every boot (introduced in v21). Now bind-mounts the host's existing /mnt/boot mountpoint instead, same pattern already used by apply_discard_fix. Root-caused and verified via live bisection: reproduced 3/3 with the old code, 0/3 with the fix, on the same hardware/SD card.
